mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 7.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening demand for goods and services that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.2 million signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real disposable personal income above 2% signals an increase in consumer spending that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial jobless claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces unemployment and boosts corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200K are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals an increase in inflation that reduces purchasing power and boosts interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the GDP deflator below 1.5% signals a decrease in production costs that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining GDP deflator are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
